segments.text: This packaging is a disaster. It looks
like a boring spreadsheet and it has
zero aesthetic appeal. By all logic,
this brand would have failed instantly,
but it did not. It made 1.3 cr in just 1
month. It sold out everywhere and people
trust it more than heritage brands. But
why? Because food farmer knew the
industry's dirty secret, dark patterns.
Brands make the font pretty to distract
you from the chemicals hidden in the
tiny fourpoint font in the back. So, he
did the unthinkable. He took the boring
nutrition table and the thing liars hide
and made it the hero of the front page.
It's called radical transparency.
Designers hate it because it's ugly, but
customers love it because when the
ingredients are this big, you simply
can't lie. But for more amazing design
content, you can subscribe.
